title: The Indissoluble Bond
subtitle: By the Jordan, the Pharisees test Him on marriage, and He calls the soul to a higher continence

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the road fall away.
The Master journeys from Galilee into the borders of Judea.
Cross the Jordan with Him, and grow quiet.
gospel: After this, when the Lord Jesus had finished these words,
He departed from Galilee and came into the borders of Judea beyond the Jordan.
And great multitudes followed Him from Galilee,
And the Pharisees came to Him, tempting Him,
and saying: Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife for any cause?
He answered: only for the cause of fornication is a wife to be put away.
The bond of marriage is loosed by nothing but death;
neither man nor woman may take another while the first yet lives.
His disciples said to Him: If the case of a man with his wife be so, it is not expedient to marry.
He said to them: Not all receive this word of continence,
but they to whom it is given.
behold: See the two kinds of mind that draw near to Him.
The multitudes come together, that they may be taught and their sick be healed;
these the devotion of holy love brought to Him.
The Pharisees approach, that by tempting they may seize and deceive the Teacher of truth;
these the goad of envy drove on.
Behold how He frames His answer to slip past their snare:
the writ of divorce Moses once permitted for the hardness and malice of their hearts,
not as a thing good and lawful, but to avoid a greater evil —
lest, hating their wives, men fall to murder.
God is not contrary to Himself, to command one thing before and another now.
Moses did not order this by commanding, nor by granting, nor by indulging;
but by bearing with it and permitting it, that worse things should not be done.
consider: Understand the bond: it is loosed by nothing but death.
The Lord punishes fornication alone as cause of separation,
bidding all other troubles be borne for the faith of marriage and for chastity.
Hear Jerome: A wife is a heavy burden, if save for fornication alone it is not lawful to put her away.
What if she be drunken, if wrathful, if a glutton, if a wanderer,
if sickly, if a brawler, if foul-tongued?
Such a one is to be feared, and, willing or unwilling, she must be borne.
For when we were free, of our own will we made ourselves servants.
And Chrysostom: It is lighter to war against concupiscence, against oneself, than against an evil woman.
Now hear the higher word, not of necessity but of counsel:
continence from every act of the flesh.
Not all receive this word, for not all are willing to receive it;
the lustful man hears it, and it displeases him.
But to whom it is given by God, by a special gift —
for the state is lofty, to which we have no strength but by grace.
By the wife who is not put away except for fornication, understand the soul,
who never puts away the soul but for the fornication of sin,
through which Christ withdraws from us.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, who hast exhorted us to continence,
and bestow on me, the frail one, chastity of mind and body;
and Thou who didst will the little children to come to Thee,
and laying Thy hands upon them didst bless them,
grant to me, a wretch, that I may be a little child in my own eyes,
so that I may find grace in the eyes of Thy majesty;
and what the little ones possess by nature, may I obtain in mercy by Thy grace,
that by the merits of all the little and humble ones who please Thee,
I, the last of all, with Thee as ruler, with Thee as guide,
may deserve to come to Thee,
and be rewarded by Thee with blessing in the heavens. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Una ergo solummodo carnalis est causa, id est fornicatio, una spiritualis, hoc est timor Dei, ut uxor dimittatur; nulla autem causa est ut, vivente ea, quæ relicta est, alia ducatur.
latinJewel.english: There is therefore one carnal cause only, that is fornication, and one spiritual, that is the fear of God, for which a wife may be put away; but there is no cause at all that, while she who is forsaken yet lives, another should be taken.
